description_long: This is a single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined fuselage and a glass canopy cockpit situated just behind the propeller. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the midsection of the fuselage, with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral angles. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the cockpit canopy appears to be enclosed with multiple window panels. The surface is smooth and appears to be constructed primarily from metal, typical of mid-20th-century aircraft. No landing gear is visible in the current views, suggesting it may be retracted or not modeled.
description: A streamlined, single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with an enclosed glass canopy cockpit and a conventional tail. The surface is smooth and metallic, with straight wings and a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, the full wingspan, and the glass canopy cockpit behind the nose.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, the cockpit canopy, and the conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the straight wings extending from the fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, showing the propeller, cockpit, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings, no visible winglets, and no pronounced dihedral or anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible in current views.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel glass canopy, likely single-seat.
